$schema_note: Phase 4 — Events, Organizations, Advising, Evaluation. module-registry.json only defines these at summary level, so this file is intentionally shallow. Expand each work package to the same depth as the Phase 1 files (02-08) when Phase 4 is actually scheduled.

work_packages.name: Events
work_packages.goal: Event creation with QR attendance reuse (event sessions).

stub_tasks.title: Expand into full WBS (event entity, non-offering-anchored sessions, audience targeting, turnout reporting)
stub_tasks.notes: docs/index.html already has a working Events prototype (DB.events, .evt-card, .live-feed patterns) — a strong starting reference for this phase's UI.

open_questions: Is class_sessions reused directly for events (schedule_id nullable already supports this) or does an events module get its own session table?
Multi-day events — single QR session or one per day?

work_packages.name: Organizations

stub_tasks.title: Expand into full WBS (org entity, membership/officer roles, org-scoped announcements/events integration)

open_questions: Does this need its own RBAC layer (org officer role) on top of the 5 existing roles?

work_packages.name: Advising

stub_tasks.title: Expand into full WBS (consultation scheduling, adviser notes, at-risk hand-off from P1.REPORTS.BE03)
stub_tasks.notes: students.adviser_faculty_id already exists in the Phase 1 schema — this module extends an existing relationship, not a new one.

open_questions: Are adviser notes visible to the student, or staff-only?
Does at-risk notification (P1.REPORTS.BE03) route directly into an advising case here?

work_packages.name: Evaluation

stub_tasks.title: Expand into full WBS (form builder or fixed form, anonymity model, submission window tied to academic_terms, analytics/reporting)

open_questions: Must responses be anonymous to faculty, and if so what does that mean for the audit_logs append-only actor tracking convention used everywhere else?
Fixed institutional evaluation form or a form builder?
